element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Classic tribe preserves these skills with original behavior
Used to overcome obstacles and traverse hazards
Create crossings and establish safe passages
Control lemming flow and group behavior
Direct propulsion of affected lemmings and mechanism activation

element_introduction_order.element: Tribe-specific skill variations (43 total new skills)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Highland, Cavelem, Circus, and other tribes
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Distributed across worlds; some skills more frequent in specific tribes
Navigate around or disable via skills
Require coordination of multiple lemmings and path planning
Silver/bronze available; gold required for talisman piece

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Upon clearing each world with gold medals

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully guiding a lemming to exit
Clearing all 10 levels of a tribe (world transition)
Practice mode availability for skill experimentation
typical_tension_triggers: Lemming attrition (carried over between world levels)
No variable completion requirements (all-or-nothing for access)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive difficulty curve per tribe; player agency in world selection order; time-pressure-based tension cycles within each level

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Practice mode provides four alternate level layouts for skill experimentation; non-mandatory skill usage patterns

difficulty_curve.early_game: Highland and initial tribes introduce basic mechanics; single lemming survival only required; relatively forgiving trap density
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Cavelem, Circus, and mid-tier tribes increase complexity; terrain manipulation becomes critical; trap density and multiple entry/exit combinations escalate
difficulty_curve.late_game: Final tribes demand advanced skill sequencing; limited skill uses force optimization; multiple lemmings require precision coordination; talisman piece pressure mounts
notable_spikes: Transition from single-solution levels to multi-path puzzles (mid-world boundaries)
First encounter with behavioral-modifier skill chains (around tribe 4-5)
Talisman piece requirement (all 10 levels per tribe must reach gold)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each tribe introduces or emphasizes different skill subsets (mobility, terrain, behavior) without hard exclusivity; allows early-game skill mastery to carry forward
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Highland tribe levels 1-3 (basic foundation); Cavelem introduces mining/carving skills

notable_level_patterns.description: World-level lemming pool (60 per world) persists across all 10 levels; forces player to revisit earlier levels if insufficient lemmings remain; creates meta-level puzzle constraint
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any world where late levels are designed assuming full or near-full lemming complement

notable_level_patterns.description: Gold medal (highest lemming survival) required for each level to secure talisman piece; creates secondary completion loop beyond basic level access
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All 120 levels include gold/silver/bronze rankings; gold mandatory for world completion

notable_level_patterns.description: Twelve independent tribal worlds can be tackled in player-chosen order; reduces frustration on stuck levels; increases replayability and strategic pacing control
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Main menu offers free selection of all 12 tribes after initial world unlock

notable_level_patterns.description: Unlimited, player-controlled fan adds real-time mechanical layer; affects Ballooner, Surfer, and level mechanisms; requires skill timing and positioning awareness
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any level featuring Ballooner or Surfer skills with wind-powered mechanisms

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Lemmings 2: The Tribes SNES)
